subject: Sports
title: The Three-Point Line
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Three-Point Line.

hook.text: Before 1979, every basket in the NBA was worth the same—two points. Then one line changed everything.

buildup.text: The ABA introduced the three-point line in 1967 to add excitement. The NBA adopted it in 1979.

discovery.text: At first, players rarely shot threes. In 1980, teams averaged just 2.8 three-point attempts per game.

twist.text: By 2024, teams attempted over 35 threes per game. The three-pointer reshaped the entire sport.

climax.text: Stephen Curry's long-range shooting made the three-pointer the most important shot in modern basketball.

punchline.text: One painted arc on the floor revolutionized how basketball is played.

quiz.question: When did the NBA adopt the three-point line?
